title: Closure of Strait of Hormuz is 'greatest global energy security threat in history,' warns IEA chief
subhead: Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency, does not rule out another release of part of the member states' strategic oil reserves to ease prices.

title: French prosecutors flag possible manipulation of X stock prices by Musk to US authorities

Macron's role in Paris mayor election comes under scrutiny
subhead: Socialist frontrunner Emmanuel Grégoire accused President Emmanuel Macron of having intervened to obtain the withdrawal of far-right candidate Sarah Knafo in favor of conservative Rachida Dati. Macron firmly denied.

title: France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
subhead: As the Charles de Gaulle and its strike group approach the Middle East, Le Monde identified a French sailor using the Strava fitness application in the Mediterranean Sea. This security flaw remains unaddressed despite our previous revelations.

title: Chuck Norris, king of martial arts and Reagan-era action movies, has died
subhead: The American actor best known for the hit series 'Walker, Texas Ranger' died on Friday, at the age of 86.

subhead: The Europeans' refusal to join Washington in the current Middle East war shows that the transatlantic rift is deeper than the one seen during the 2003 Iraq crisis, observes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

title: How Budapest became a hub for Western nationalists under Viktor Orban
subhead: The Hungarian capital, popular among hard-right circles in the West, is home to a number of think tanks and conferences promoting the image of Prime Minister Viktor Orban with the approach of the legislative elections on April 12.

title: Putin conducts a 'special musical operation' in Europe
subhead: Barred from the West, Valery Gergiev, maestro at the helm of Russia's two leading opera houses and cultural ambassador for Vladimir Putin, is seeking a return to conducting in Europe's major concert halls. In this cultural arena, Moscow is shifting from 'soft power' to 'sharp power.'
